word: G4851 - συμφέρω - symphérō - soom-fer'-o - Be better for

sections.paragraph:
- Original: συμφέρω

- Transliteration: Sumphero

- Phonetic: soom-fer'-o

- Definition:

1. to bear or bring together

2. to bear together or at the same time

a. to carry with others

b. to collect or contribute in order to help

c. to help, be profitable, be expedient

- Origin: from G4862 and G5342 (including its alternate)

- TDNT entry: 10:09,1

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G4862 and G5342 (including its alternate); to bear together (contribute) that is (literally) to collect or (figuratively) to conduce; especially (neuter participle as noun) advantage: - be better for bring together be expedient (for) be good (be) profit (-able for).
